findings.summary: Two independent code paths produce JSON+Markdown error reports with nearly identical structure
evidence: error_aggregator.py:198-222 ErrorAggregator.save_report() writes error_summary.json and error_summary.md
pipeline_reporter.py:451-485 save_error_summary() independently writes the same two files with the same categorization logic
Both functions produce by_type grouping and markdown formatting for the same error domain
findings.suggestion: Eliminate save_error_summary() in pipeline_reporter.py and route all error report generation through ErrorAggregator.save_report(); or remove ErrorAggregator.save_report() and delegate to the standalone function — pick one path and delete the other

findings.summary: Three-location PDF fallback search logic is implemented twice in output_validator.py
evidence: validate_copied_outputs() lines 36-111 implements: check root dir -> check pdf/ dir -> check source projects/ dir
validate_output_structure() lines 416-472 implements the same three-location fallback with near-identical path construction
Both functions independently construct repo_root via path_parts.index('output') and source_pdf_dir = repo_root / 'projects' / project_name / 'output' / 'pdf'
findings.suggestion: Extract a single _find_combined_pdf(output_dir, project_name) helper that returns the resolved Path or None; both validators call it and act on the result

findings.summary: validate_copied_outputs is a 136-line function mixing PDF search, directory iteration, and diagnostic logging
evidence: output_validator.py:16-171: single function performs project name extraction (lines 38-48), three-location PDF search (lines 49-125), directory presence loop (lines 127-169)
The three-location PDF search block alone spans 76 lines with nested conditionals
findings.suggestion: Extract _find_combined_pdf() for the search logic and _check_subdirectories() for the directory loop; validate_copied_outputs becomes a short compositor calling both

findings.summary: Blank line inside StreamingProgress._display before eta_str assignment is a residue of removed code
evidence: logging_progress.py:280-282: 'if self.current > 0 and elapsed > 0:' block ends, then blank line, then 'eta_str = f" | ETA: ..."' — the blank line suggests a deleted rate-limiting branch was not cleaned up
findings.suggestion: Remove the orphaned blank line at line 281 to restore control-flow clarity in _display

findings.summary: Module-level mutable singleton in error_aggregator.py is an implicit hidden dependency inconsistent with instance-based composition elsewhere
evidence: error_aggregator.py:291-303: _global_aggregator module-level variable with get_error_aggregator() lazy-init accessor
reset_error_aggregator() exists only to clear this global for testing, confirming it is a test-visible side effect
All other infrastructure state is passed as constructor arguments or function parameters
findings.suggestion: Remove the global singleton; callers that need a shared aggregator should construct one instance and pass it explicitly, or use a context object

findings.summary: pipeline_reporter.py combines data assembly, HTML generation, Markdown generation, JSON serialization, validation reporting, and error reporting in one module
evidence: pipeline_reporter.py exports: generate_pipeline_report (data assembly), save_pipeline_report (multi-format I/O), generate_html_report (HTML rendering), _generate_pipeline_markdown (MD rendering), generate_validation_report, generate_validation_markdown, generate_performance_report, save_error_summary, generate_error_markdown — 9 distinct responsibilities
generate_html_report is 150 lines of inline CSS + HTML f-string construction in a reporting data module
findings.suggestion: Split into pipeline_report_data.py (PipelineReport dataclass + generate_pipeline_report), pipeline_report_renderers.py (HTML/Markdown rendering functions), and keep save_* I/O thin wrappers; move validation and error report generation to their respective modules

findings.summary: ErrorAggregator.get_actionable_fixes() uses a string-keyed if/elif chain that should be data-driven
evidence: error_aggregator.py:136-194: explicit if error_type == 'test_failure' / elif error_type == 'validation_error' / elif error_type == 'stage_failure' / else pattern — adding a new error type requires editing this function
Each branch produces a structurally identical dict with priority, issue, actions, documentation keys
findings.suggestion: Define a dict mapping error type strings to (priority, actions, documentation) tuples; get_actionable_fixes() looks up the type and falls back to a generic entry, eliminating the branch chain
